questions.statement:
<p><strong>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</strong></p>

<p>I tried hardly to teach her (1)/ my sign language, but she (2)/ was dull and lazy. (3)/ No error (4)</p>

options: at receiving no answer from you /

questions.statement:
<p>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>Making and to get rid of (1)/ excess food accounts for (2)/ around 8-10 percent&nbsp;of greenhouse gas emissions. (3)/ No error (4)</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Find the part of the sentence that contains an error:</span></p>

<p>(1) The internal combustion engine&rsquo;s contribution to atmospheric carbon dioxide levels / (2)&nbsp;is arguably more significant than any technological innovation / (3)&nbsp;of the industrial era, with the possible exception of the coal-fired steam engine / (4)&nbsp;which powered the initial expansion of the global manufacturing sector.</p>

<p>Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</p>

<p>I shall be playing for four hours.</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Identify the part of the sentence that contains a mistake.</strong></p>

<p>My father&nbsp;<span class="math-tex">\(\frac{\text {is}}{(\rm A)}\)</span> one of&nbsp;<span class="math-tex">\(\frac{\rm those~people}{(\rm B)} \frac{\rm who}{\rm (C)} \frac{\rm doesn't~like}{(\rm D)}\)</span> football.</p>

<p>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>The rabbit lead the boy towards the outskirts of the city.&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>In the following question, one part of the sentence may have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the option corresponding to it. (Avoid punctuation errors)</strong></p>

<p>(A) All the furnitures have been / (B) sent to the old office / (C) located in a town. / (D) NO ERROR</p>

<p>The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</p>

<p>The workers called out / the strike because / the management acceded to / all their demands.&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Direction: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</strong></p>

<p>Janet has / a pet dog / who are / very smart.</p>

questions.statement:
<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;"><u>Direction</u>:</span><span style="font-weight: 700;">&nbsp;Find out which part has an error and mark it as your answer. If there is no error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer. The underlined part of the sentence doesn&#39;t contain any error.</span></p>

<p style="">I <u>should have</u> said (A) /&nbsp; something to her (B) / if&nbsp;I saw her earlier. (C) / No error (D)</p>

<p><strong>The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don&rsquo;t find any error, mark &lsquo;No error&rsquo; as your answer.</strong></p>

<p>He had been improve / his communication skills in order / to shine in his career.&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style=" font-weight: 700;">Direction:&nbsp;</span><span style="">Read the sentence to find out whether there is any error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence.</span></p>

<p>&quot;We feel like we presented/ a case that showed through evidence and testimony/ that Mr. Rhodes did not committed/ the crime of seditious conspiracy,&quot; Tarpley said.</p>
options: <span style="">that Mr. Rhodes did not committed</span> /
<span style="">a case that showed through evidence and testimony</span> /
<span style="">the crime of seditious conspiracy,&quot; Tarpley said.</span> /

questions.statement:
<p>In the below question, a part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose &#39;No Improvement&#39; option.</p>

<p>The Commonwealth remains a great platform for development aid, democratic values and educational opportunities, but its relevance is unlikely to increase (unless it adopt a more) egalitarian and inclusive attitude.</p>

<p><strong>Directions: Find the wrong part by its number from the sentence given below :</strong></p>

<p>The husband asked his wife (a)&nbsp;to wait for him near the gate (b)&nbsp;until (c)&nbsp;he did not come back (d).</p>

<p>Identify the segment in the sentence, which contains the grammatical error.</p>

<p>You were /not at home/ when I come/ to your house yesterday.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">In the given options&nbsp;find the verb that agrees with the subject of the given sentence</span>.</p>

<p>Locally produced goods ______&nbsp;the advantage of shorter supply chains. This serves to be a major advantage for locally-made products, thereby increasing their competitiveness.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Direction: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don&rsquo;t find any error, mark &lsquo;No error&rsquo; as your answer.</strong></p>

<p>Children were prohibited to attend / the military parade in which / deadly weapons were displayed.</p>

<p><strong>The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don&rsquo;t find any error, mark &lsquo;No error&rsquo; as your answer.</strong></p>

<p>Saleem has a tendency to switch / off his mobile when things / are not favourable for him.&nbsp;</p>
options: Saleem has a tendency to switch /
